name: mybrain
description: Personal semantic thought storage and retrieval with vector embeddings and ltree scoping
long_description: MyBrain is a personal knowledge base with semantic search. It stores thoughts in PostgreSQL with vector embeddings (pgvector + ltree), making everything searchable by meaning rather than keyword. Runs as a local stdio MCP server. Supports OpenRouter (cloud) or Ollama (local) for embeddings.

database_url.title: PostgreSQL connection URL
database_url.description: postgres://user:pass@host:5432/dbname — must have pgvector + ltree extensions installed. Local Postgres, Docker container, or remote (RDS) all work.

brain_scope.title: Brain name / default scope
brain_scope.description: ltree namespace stamped on every thought captured without an explicit scope (e.g. 'kyroco', 'personal', 'work.research'). This is what differentiates one brain from another when multiple repos share a database. Comma-separate for multiple scopes.

embedding_provider.title: Embedding provider
embedding_provider.description: openrouter (cloud, requires API key) or ollama (local, free, requires Ollama running on this Mac). Leave default unless you set up Ollama.

embedding_api_key.title: OpenRouter API key
embedding_api_key.description: Used only when provider is openrouter. Get one at https://openrouter.ai/keys. Leave blank if using Ollama.

ollama_host.title: Ollama host URL
ollama_host.description: Used only when provider is ollama. Default works for a local Ollama install (brew install ollama && ollama serve).

ollama_model.title: Ollama embedding model
ollama_model.description: Used only when provider is ollama. Default produces 1536-dim vectors matching the default schema. Run `ollama pull <model>` first.
